description: Dependency doctrine (2026-07-14): pin versions (rangeStrategy pin), pin the behaviors we rely on with tests, then bump as eagerly as possible — behind GREEN CI, so we are always first to know when something breaks. automerge waits for tests by DEFAULT: there is deliberately NO global ignoreTests (it previously merged BEFORE CI, inverting the doctrine — breakage landed on main instead of being caught in the PR). The one exception to eagerness is npm supply chain (see the npm rule): tests cannot catch a malicious publish, so time is the guard.

packageRules.description: npm supply chain is a DIFFERENT threat model: tests catch breakage, not a malicious/compromised publish (it passes every test and still backdoors). Time is the only defense — a bad release is typically yanked within days. Hold every npm update 7 days before it is eligible to automerge. The deliberate exception to eager bumping.
solaris vmactions confs mix toolchain variants (gcc/clang) into one namespace; track only the gcc line the leg is built around.

customManagers.description: ci-guest-version pins in the tjs leg manifest, annotated in place with `// renovate: datasource=... depName=...` on the preceding line. JSONata transforms proven against the live APIs 2026-07-11.

cpa-freebsd-x86-64.description: OS versions = the x86-64 image assets of the newest cross-platform-actions/freebsd-builder release (Renovate's built-in github-releases datasource sees only the BUILDER's own version tags).

cpa-netbsd-x86-64.description: OS versions = the x86-64 image assets of the newest cross-platform-actions/netbsd-builder release.

cpa-omnios-x86-64.description: OS versions = the x86-64 image assets of the newest cross-platform-actions/omnios-builder release.

vmactions-solaris.description: vmactions guest versions are conf filenames in the action repo.
